title: The Third Conditional
description: Talk about unreal situations in the past: the conditional perfect (habría hablado), the pluperfect subjunctive (hubiera hablado) and the si hubiera… habría… pattern.

cards.notes: **Condicional de haber** (*habría*) + **participio**: habría, habrías, habría, habríamos, habríais, habrían.

cards.back: If I had known, I would have come.
cards.notes: Third conditional (unreal past): **Si + pluscuamperfecto de subjuntivo, condicional compuesto**.

cards.back: in the result clause, habría and hubiera are both accepted
cards.notes: Colloquially the result clause often uses *hubiera* too: *Si hubiera sabido, **hubiera venido***. Both are correct.

cards.front: Si hubiera estudiado, ahora tendría el título.
cards.back: If I had studied, I would now have the degree.
cards.notes: Mixed conditional: past condition (*hubiera estudiado*) → present result (*tendría*).

cards.back: remember: the participle never changes after haber
cards.notes: After *haber* the participle is invariable: *habría **dicho***, *hubiera **hecho***, *habría **escrito***. Mind the irregulars (dicho, hecho, escrito, visto, puesto, vuelto).
*deber/poder + haber + participio* expresses past reproach or missed possibility.

steps.title: Two compound forms
steps.body:
# Building the pieces

Both tenses are **haber + participio**; only the form of *haber* changes.

## Conditional perfect — what *would have* happened

> **condicional de haber + participio**

*habría, habrías, habría, habríamos, habríais, habrían* + participio

- *Yo **habría venido**.* — I would have come.

## Pluperfect subjunctive — the unreal *had done*

> **imperfecto de subjuntivo de haber + participio**

*hubiera/hubiese, hubieras, hubiera…* + participio

- *Si yo **hubiera sabido**…* — If I had known…

The participle is **invariable** after *haber*; watch the irregulars: *dicho, hecho, escrito, visto, puesto, vuelto*.

steps.title: The third conditional
steps.body:
# Unreal situations in the past

> **Si + pluscuamperfecto de subjuntivo, condicional compuesto**

- *Si **hubiera sabido**, **habría venido**.* — If I had known, I would have come.

In the **result** clause, Spanish accepts both *habría* and *hubiera*:

- *Si hubiera sabido, **hubiera venido**.* (equally correct)

## Mixed conditional

A past condition can lead to a **present** result — then use the simple conditional:

- *Si **hubiera estudiado**, ahora **tendría** el título.* — If I had studied, I would now have the degree.

## A useful structure

- *Deberías **haber** estudiado.* — You should have studied. (reproach about the past)

exercise.prompt: Match each Spanish form with its English meaning.
Fill in the pluperfect subjunctive of saber (yo): "If I had known…"
exercise.sentence: Si ___ sabido la verdad, habría actuado de otra forma.

exercise.prompt: Fill in the conditional of haber (yo): "…I would have come."
exercise.sentence: Si me lo hubieras dicho, ___ venido.
exercise.hint: conditional of haber (habría) + venido; hubiera is also accepted here.

exercise.prompt: Translate: *If you had studied, you would have passed the exam.* (estudiar / aprobar)
accept: Si hubieras estudiado, habrías aprobado el examen.
si hubieras estudiado, habrías aprobado el examen
Si hubieras estudiado, habrías aprobado el examen.
Si hubieras estudiado, hubieras aprobado el examen.
distractors: Si habrías estudiado, habrías aprobado el examen.
Si hubieras estudiado, aprobarías el examen.
Si estudiarías, habrías aprobado el examen.
